description: Unit/integration test host bootstrap command planning: minimal k3s install/start, image import, storage apply, control-plane apply, and setup URL reporting happen in the expected order and are idempotent.
Manifest render test verifies the appliance control-plane namespace, workloads, service, exposure mechanism, RBAC, and required secrets/config are present and scoped as expected.
ISO staging test verifies baked control-plane manifests, image archive(s), local storage manifest, and fallback scripts are present at installed-appliance paths rather than repository-relative paths.

Control-plane API workflow test validates setup input submission, release/runtime selection persistence, initial tenant/admin Secret creation, Flux/app bootstrap configuration, and resumable state across a simulated pod restart.
Status resilience test verifies the Kubernetes-hosted status API remains reachable and reports actionable blockers when Alga PSA HelmRelease/pods/bootstrap job are missing or unhealthy.
UI build/smoke test verifies the setup and status pages render from the Kubernetes-packaged artifact and preserve the existing tokenized setup/status flow.
Host fallback test verifies reapplying the baked control-plane bundle is safe on an already bootstrapped new install and does not delete app data or secrets.
Fresh-install smoke test boots a newly built Ubuntu appliance ISO, reaches Kubernetes-hosted setup UI without registry/GitHub dependency, submits tenant/admin setup, and verifies login-ready status.
Documentation/runbook review test verifies support docs describe the new bootstrap layers, fallback command, logs to inspect, and new-install-only rollout boundary.
